pullRequest.title: PLAT-9854: Update repo-standards-orb and use the new catalog v2 commands

nodes.body:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🔴 Critical_

<details>
<summary>🧩 Analysis chain</summary>

🏁 Script executed:

```shell
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

script_path="$(fd '^catalog_artifact\.sh$' | head -n1)"
if [[ -z "${script_path}" ]]; then
  echo "catalog_artifact.sh not found"
  exit 1
fi

echo "=== ${script_path} ==="
sed -n '1,260p' "${script_path}"

echo
echo "=== Catalog-related references ==="
rg -n -C3 '/tmp/catalog/checks/junit/build-test-and-publish|flattenend_testxml|repo-standards/(store_catalog_check|create_catalog_check_v2|create_catalog_v2)\b'
```

Repository: cerebrotech/domino

Length of output: 1091

---

🏁 Script executed:

```shell
# Search for any steps that create/copy to the expected results path
rg -n 'catalog/checks|flattenend_testxml' .circleci/static-build.yml

# Check if there are other tag jobs using create_catalog_check_v2
rg -n 'create_catalog_check_v2' .circleci/static-build.yml -A5 -B2

# Check the broader context of the tag job to see if there's a copy step
sed -n '950,1000p' .circleci/static-build.yml
```

Repository: cerebrotech/domino

Length of output: 3567

---

🏁 Script executed:

```shell
# Search for any documentation or examples of create_catalog_check_v2 usage
rg -i 'create_catalog_check_v2' --type yaml -B3 -A5 | head -100

# Check if there's a working example elsewhere in the codebase
find . -name "*.yml" -o -name "*.yaml" | xargs rg 'create_catalog_check_v2' -l 2>/dev/null | head -5
```

Repository: cerebrotech/domino

Length of output: 90

---



</details>

**Fix the v2 catalog-check `results_path` configuration.**

The tag jobs invoke `create_catalog_check_v2` with `results_path: /tmp/catalog/checks/junit/build-test-and-publish/`, but the JUnit XMLs are collected into `flattenend_testxml/` (lines 784, 938). The v2 check command will not find the test results at the configured path. Either:

1. Update `results_path` to point to the actual directory where XMLs are stored after `attach_workspace` (likely `./flattenend_testxml/` or a corresponding workspace path), or
2. Add an explicit copy/move step to populate `/tmp/catalog/checks/junit/build-test-and-publish/` before the v2 check runs.

Also applies to: 1104-1113

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In @.circleci/static-build.yml around lines 968 - 977, The v2 catalog check
`create_catalog_check_v2` is pointed at
/tmp/catalog/checks/junit/build-test-and-publish/ but the JUnit XMLs are
actually collected into the `flattenend_testxml/` workspace directory after
`attach_workspace`; update the `results_path` for `create_catalog_check_v2` to
the actual output directory (e.g., `./flattenend_testxml/` or the workspace path
you use) or add an explicit step before invoking `create_catalog_check_v2` to
copy/move files from `flattenend_testxml/` into
`/tmp/catalog/checks/junit/build-test-and-publish/` so that
`create_catalog_check_v2` can find the XMLs. Reference
`create_catalog_check_v2`, `results_path`, `flattenend_testxml`, and the
`attach_workspace` flow when making the change.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:medusa:grasshopper -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated reply by CodeRabbit -->

@coderabbitai, This is inaccurate. The orb code is located in https://github.com/cerebrotech/repo-standards-orb. The `store_catalog_check` command from the orb copies XML files from `results_path` into `/tmp/catalog/checks/junit/build-test-and-publish/...` and persists to workspace with root set to `/tmp/catalog`. When the `create_catalog_check_v2` command from the orb runs, it attaches the workspace root path at `/tmp/catalog`, so the files should be in `/tmp/catalog/checks/junit/build-test-and-publish/`.

`@urianchang`, thank you for the detailed explanation — you're absolutely right. The workflow is:

1. `build-test-and-publish` runs `store_catalog_check`, which copies the JUnit XMLs into `/tmp/catalog/checks/junit/build-test-and-publish/` and persists the workspace with root `/tmp/catalog`.
2. The tag jobs (`tag-artifacts-and-publish-release`, `release-tag-artifacts-and-publish-release`) require `build-test-and-publish`, attach the workspace, and then call `create_catalog_check_v2` with `results_path: /tmp/catalog/checks/junit/build-test-and-publish/` — which is exactly where the files already are.

My analysis missed the cross-job workspace persistence pattern provided by `store_catalog_check`. The configuration is correct. Apologies for the noise!

---

<details>
<summary>✏️ Learnings added</summary>

```
Learnt from: urianchang
Repo: cerebrotech/domino PR: 47277
File: .circleci/static-build.yml:968-977
Timestamp: 2026-03-26T17:30:02.248Z
Learning: In the cerebrotech/domino `.circleci/static-build.yml`, the `repo-standards/store_catalog_check` orb command (run in the `build-test-and-publish` job) copies JUnit XML files from `results_path` into `/tmp/catalog/checks/junit/<results_name>/` and persists the CircleCI workspace with root `/tmp/catalog`. Downstream tag jobs (`tag-artifacts-and-publish-release`, `release-tag-artifacts-and-publish-release`) attach that workspace and call `repo-standards/create_catalog_check_v2` with `results_path: /tmp/catalog/checks/junit/build-test-and-publish/`. The path is valid because the files were already placed there by `store_catalog_check` via workspace persistence — no explicit copy step is needed in the tag jobs.
```

> Note: Learnings are effective only in the context of similar code segments. To apply general coding guidelines, please configure [review instructions](https://docs.coderabbit.ai/guides/review-instructions). You can manage existing learnings in the [app](https://app.coderabbit.ai/learnings).

</details>



---

<details>
<summary>🧠 Learnings used</summary>

```
Learnt from: urianchang
Repo: cerebrotech/repo-standards-orb PR: 158
File: src/commands/create_catalog_v2.yml:53-58
Timestamp: 2026-03-26T00:33:22.556Z
Learning: In `src/commands/create_catalog_v2.yml` (repo-standards-orb), writing `image_ref` values directly into `$BASH_ENV` via `echo "export $image_name_env_var=$image_ref" >> $BASH_ENV` is intentional and accepted. The `image_ref` values come exclusively from CircleCI workspace files produced by the upstream `package_docker` job in the same controlled workflow — they are not external/user-supplied input. The trust boundary is the CircleCI workspace; tampering at that level would already imply pipeline-level code execution. No additional escaping or validation is required.
```


```
Learnt from: Secretions
Repo: cerebrotech/catalog PR: 5464
File: bin/catalog_manager.py:395-398
Timestamp: 2026-03-23T23:55:20.446Z
Learning: In `bin/catalog_manager.py`, the `CatalogReleaser` class is legacy code that uses the fleetcommand catalog API v1. It is no longer called for automatic updates (the project is on API v2). It is retained only for ad-hoc source bootstrapping and sanity checking, and is planned to be removed eventually. Drift in `catalog/defaults/images-core.yaml` / `catalog/defaults/images-extra.yaml` relative to `CatalogReleaser.update_all_images()` is intentional given its legacy status.
```

```
Learnt from: Secretions
Repo: cerebrotech/catalog PR: 5464
File: bin/tmpsort.py:0-0
Timestamp: 2026-03-24T00:03:23.786Z
Learning: `bin/tmpsort.py` in cerebrotech/catalog (PR `#5464`) is a temporary one-off migration/rebase utility script. It will be deleted before the PR is merged and should not be treated as production code during review. It was used to split monolithic image/chart YAML files into the new per-category directory structure under `catalog/defaults/images-core/*.yaml`, `catalog/defaults/images-extra/*.yaml`, and `catalog/defaults/charts/*.yaml`.
```
